reasoning: The demand to delete photos of a specific guest solely due to their gender identity serves no legitimate property interest (like safety or liability) and is purely discriminatory. A reasonable person would immediately recognize the injustice of erasing a family member from wedding photos based on bigotry, especially when the photographer is contracted by the couple.

The injustice is obvious because the rule's stated rationale (noise abatement) is factually inapplicable to the prohibited item (a silent battery), rendering the prohibition irrational. A reasonable person would immediately recognize that banning a silent medical necessity under the guise of noise control is absurd, regardless of private property rights.
